summary: R3 validation confirms the repaired first-screen UX evidence now satisfies the recovery claim at both 1440x900 and 1280x720. The revised smoke asserts actual viewport intersection and visible area for the orientation card, actionable task rail summary, map surface, and map legend, and the regenerated screenshots show the map and legend above the fold in the smaller viewport as well as the desktop viewport. This is validation evidence only and does not approve or reopen M7 content lock.

tests_run.result: No whitespace or patch formatting issues.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts -g "M7 task rail is first-screen actionable|M7 UX recovery smoke" --project=chromium
tests_run.result: 2 passed; the viewport-visible assertions and regenerated screenshots both passed.

Validated commit 523bd6364a82a2aba0f7a4fed1889792762579eb: the repair touched apps/web/src/styles/shell.css, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, the regenerated screenshots, and the routing/task state artifacts for the client repair.

decisions.result: Kept the validation focused on viewport-visible first-screen evidence and did not reopen content lock.
Classified the client repair as REVIEW because the smaller viewport now visibly contains the map, map legend, orientation card, and actionable task rail summary.

risks: This remains a validation artifact, not a product-owner content-lock approval.
Future UX polish may still be warranted for dense 1280-class desktop layouts, but it no longer blocks the evidence claim.
requested_action: Review the repaired viewport-visible evidence and route to QA if accepted. Do not approve or reopen formal content lock from this validation task.
